title: Homological Mirror Symmetry for singularities of type $T_{pqr}$
speaker: Ailsa Keating
abstract: We present some homological mirror symmetry statements for the singularities of type $T_{p,q,r}$. Loosely, these are one level of complexity up from so-called 'simple' singularities, of types $A$, $D$ and $E$. We will consider some symplectic invariants of the real four-dimensional Milnor fibres of these singularities, and explain how they correspond to coherent sheaves on certain blow-ups of the projective space $\mathbb P^2$, as suggested notably by Gross-Hacking-Keel.

proof_stages.label: Cusp singularities and dual cusp background
proof_stages.summary: The talk begins by defining cusp singularities via resolutions whose exceptional locus is a cycle of transversely intersecting projective lines, recalling Looijenga's dual cusp construction and its Gross-Hacking-Keel mirror-symmetry interpretation.

proof_stages.label: Affine T_{pqr} singularities and Milnor fibers
proof_stages.summary: The affine cusp singularities are identified with the hypersurface singularities T_{pqr}, given by an equation of the form x^p+y^q+z^r+a xyz=0 with 1/p+1/q+1/r<1, and their symplectic-side object is the exact Milnor fiber obtained by smoothing the equation.

proof_stages.label: Dual algebraic surface Y_{pqr} with boundary cycle D
proof_stages.summary: The mirror complex-side pair is constructed by repeatedly blowing up three collinear points on the toric boundary of P2, producing a surface Y_{pqr} whose boundary D is a three-component cycle with self-intersections 1-p, 1-q, and 1-r.

proof_stages.label: Good Lefschetz fibration on the Milnor fiber
proof_stages.summary: A specially chosen Lefschetz fibration pi on the Milnor fiber is described pictorially, with smooth fiber a three-punctured elliptic curve, explicit vanishing cycles, matching paths of lengths governed by p,q,r, and an intersection diagram for the resulting H_2-basis.

proof_stages.label: Three HMS equivalences and proof reduction
proof_stages.summary: The main homological mirror symmetry statements are formulated: the directed Fukaya category of pi matches D^b Coh(Y_{pqr}), the Fukaya category of the fiber matches Perf(D), and the wrapped Fukaya category of the Milnor fiber matches D^b Coh(Y_{pqr}\D), with the third to be obtained from the first two by a general localization machine.

proof_stages.label: Directed Fukaya category versus semiorthogonal decomposition
proof_stages.summary: The directed Fukaya category is defined from ordered vanishing paths and Floer complexes, computed combinatorially on the punctured elliptic curve, and matched generator-by-generator with the Beilinson and Bondal-Orlov semiorthogonal decomposition of D^b Coh(Y_{pqr}) coming from P2 and its blow-ups.

proof_stages.label: Fiber Fukaya category versus perfect complexes on D
proof_stages.summary: The Fukaya category of the three-punctured elliptic fiber is generated by four circles, and these are matched with the structure sheaf of the boundary cycle D and skyscraper sheaves at chosen points on its three components, giving the second equivalence.

proof_stages.label: Bimodule compatibility and localization to the wrapped category
proof_stages.summary: The two established equivalences are refined to compatible bimodule data, yielding corresponding monodromy/tensor functors and natural transformations; algebraic localization then gives the wrapped Fukaya category on the symplectic side and coherent sheaves on the complement Y_{pqr}\D on the algebraic side.
